summary: ANDROID 🟥 Changes to some options are not properly propagated between mobile and PC part 2
scenario: Changes to certain call-related options are not properly synchronized between mobile and PC platforms due to incorrect bitmask handling.
description_clean: Changes to some options (Calls, Hold Reminder, Incoming Call, Active Audio Tone, Answering Call, Answer/Ignore) are not properly propagated between mobile and PC when changed on one platform. Both platforms should use bitmasks correctly to ensure settings changes are propagated to every other platform.
steps: Change one of the following options on mobile: Calls, Hold Reminder, Incoming Call, Active Audio Tone, Answering Call, Answer/Ignore
Observe whether the change is reflected on the PC platform
Change one of the following options on PC: Calls, Hold Reminder, Incoming Call, Active Audio Tone, Answering Call, Answer/Ignore
Observe whether the change is reflected on the mobile platform
expected: Changes to any of the listed options (Calls, Hold Reminder, Incoming Call, Active Audio Tone, Answering Call, Answer/Ignore) should be properly propagated between mobile and PC platforms. Both platforms should use bitmasks correctly to ensure settings changes are propagated to every other platform.
actual: Changes to some options (Calls, Hold Reminder, Incoming Call, Active Audio Tone, Answering Call, Answer/Ignore) are not properly propagated between mobile and PC when changed on one platform.
